attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: {"trajectory": {"thought_0": "I need to find out which major river is closest to Buford, North Dakota. Since Buford is a small community, it may not have extensive information readily available, so I will look for details about its geographical location and nearby rivers.", "tool_name_0": "search_wikipedia", "tool_args_0": {"query": "Buford, North Dakota major river nearby"}, "observation_0": ["Buford, North Dakota | Buford is an unincorporated community in Williams County, North Dakota, United States. It is the nearest community to the Fort Union Trading Post National Historic Site. It is named for the nearby historic Fort Buford at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers.", "Fort Buford | Fort Buford was a United States Army Post at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers in Dakota Territory, present day North Dakota, and the site of Sitting Bull's surrender in 1881.", "Playford River (Northern Territory) | Playford River is a river in the Northern Territory of Australia and has a length of 406 km. The river originates near the base of Mount Morgan then flows in a southerly direction before veering west and eventually dicharging into Lake De Burgh. The Playford has three tributaries: Buchanan Creek, Desert Creek and Eastern Creek—as well as a number of permanent waterholes. These include Tobacco waterhole, Ibis waterhole, Upper and Lower Amazon Lagoon and Xmas hole."], "thought_1": "I have found that Buford, North Dakota is located near the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers. This indicates that the major river nearest to Buford is the Missouri River. I will now finalize my response.", "tool_name_1": "finish", "tool_args_1": {}, "observation_1": "Completed."}, "reasoning": "Buford, North Dakota is located near the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers. Given that the Missouri River is one of the major rivers in the United States and is specifically mentioned in relation to Buford, it is clear that the Missouri River is the nearest major river to this community.", "answer": "The nearest major river to Buford, North Dakota is the Missouri River."}

attributes.name: "search_wikipedia"
attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: ["Buford, North Dakota | Buford is an unincorporated community in Williams County, North Dakota, United States. It is the nearest community to the Fort Union Trading Post National Historic Site. It is named for the nearby historic Fort Buford at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers.", "Fort Buford | Fort Buford was a United States Army Post at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers in Dakota Territory, present day North Dakota, and the site of Sitting Bull's surrender in 1881.", "Playford River (Northern Territory) | Playford River is a river in the Northern Territory of Australia and has a length of 406 km. The river originates near the base of Mount Morgan then flows in a southerly direction before veering west and eventually dicharging into Lake De Burgh. The Playford has three tributaries: Buchanan Creek, Desert Creek and Eastern Creek—as well as a number of permanent waterholes. These include Tobacco waterhole, Ibis waterhole, Upper and Lower Amazon Lagoon and Xmas hole."]

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «Buford, North Dakota | Buford is an unincorporated community in Williams County, North Dakota, United States. It is the nearest community to the Fort Union Trading Post National Historic Site. It is named for the nearby historic Fort Buford at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers.»
[2] «Fort Buford | Fort Buford was a United States Army Post at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers in Dakota Territory, present day North Dakota, and the site of Sitting Bull's surrender in 1881.»
[3] «Playford River (Northern Territory) | Playford River is a river in the Northern Territory of Australia and has a length of 406 km. The river originates near the base of Mount Morgan then flows in a southerly direction before veering west and eventually dicharging into Lake De Burgh. The Playford has three tributaries: Buchanan Creek, Desert Creek and Eastern Creek—as well as a number of permanent waterholes. These include Tobacco waterhole, Ibis waterhole, Upper and Lower Amazon Lagoon and Xmas hole.»"}
